body.title: SensorTree node for approach-frame audit metric (versioned history, text sparkline)
body.result: Palace subtree theigors/metrics/approach_frame_audit/ created (7 nodes). Helpers in wild_igor/igor/tools/palace_metric.py: read_counter, increment_metric, append_history, parse_history, render_sparkline, render_history_sparkline. 9 tests pass (pure + DB round-trip).
body.description:
Akien 2026-04-21: '% complete might be served with a small text only line chart that shows activity toward this goal... oh! it's a metric! we have tools and a tree for that!' SensorTree (GH-281, shipped) is the home.

**Shape:** palace nodes at `theigors/metrics/approach_frame_audit/` with children:
- `rules_reviewed` — count of rule-shaped items scored
- `rules_reframed` — count successfully reframed
- `rules_kept_with_rationale` — count kept proscriptive with explicit justification
- `pct_complete` — derived counter
- `history` — one row per update batch, format: `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM | reviewed:N reframed:M` — enables text sparkline

Update mechanism: T-igor-self-audit-approach-frame and T-audit-cc-rules-approach-frame each append to history and increment counters after each batch.

Text sparkline: query `history` rows, render ASCII bar or dot-line. Callable on-demand.

**Affected files:** palace nodes under `theigors/metrics/approach_frame_audit/` (new subtree, clan.memory_palace existing table). SensorTree traversal code (locate via `find wild_igor -name 'sensor_tree*'` at sprint start) — if metrics/ branch needs registering, update that file's docstring to note metrics/ as a monitored subtree.

**Design rules:** no-sqlite (Postgres only, clan.memory_palace); no-new-memory-schemas (new path not new table); docs-in-code (if SensorTree code touched, update its docstring); test-plan-or-why-not — see below.

**Scope boundary:**
- In: create metric nodes, write increment logic, render text sparkline.
- Out: broader slow-metrics migration (T-slow-metrics-sensor-tree-pattern instantiates this as the template). Out: automated triggers (T-igor-self-audit handles its own reporting).

**Test plan:** write 3 synthetic history rows programmatically; confirm sparkline renders correctly. Simulate a batch-of-5 audit run; confirm counters increment and history appends.
